evals.prompt: A new QA engineer just joined our team. Help us create a 30-day onboarding plan for them.
expected_patterns: Week 1 checklist includes: repository clone OR environment setup OR local application running
Week 2 includes: first small win OR first real test
Week 3 includes: sprint contribution OR acceptance criteria
Week 4 includes: independence milestones OR multi-step E2E test
progressive complexity OR progressive responsibility
anti_patterns: tutorial only OR toy exercises only
no pairing OR pair programming not mentioned
no mention of documentation OR tribal knowledge
framework selection as day 1 task for existing project

evals.prompt: We need to conduct a test architecture audit for our existing Playwright suite. What should we assess and how do we report findings?
expected_patterns: test counts OR pyramid shape OR coverage distribution
flakiness OR flaky test rate OR quarantine
CI pipeline OR pipeline duration
technical debt OR skipped tests OR hardcoded data OR deprecated API
page objects OR fixture-based setup OR data factories OR conventions
strengths OR gaps OR risks OR quick wins OR strategic work
anti_patterns: focus only on coverage numbers without pyramid shape

evals.prompt: Create a Framework Walkthrough document for a Playwright project. What sections should it include and what level of detail?
expected_patterns: architecture overview OR framework version OR test runner
directory structure OR tests/ organization OR pages/ OR fixtures/ OR helpers/
How to Run Tests OR npx playwright test OR command examples
step by step guide OR new test template OR arrange-act-assert
how to debug failures OR TimeoutError OR CI vs local patterns
patterns and conventions OR page objects OR authentication OR test data OR selectors
where to find help section
anti_patterns: only document successful paths, ignore failures

evals.prompt: What questions should we ask before starting QA onboarding to understand the context?
expected_patterns: new QA engineer OR developer contributing to tests OR experience level
solo QA OR joining existing team
existing test framework OR framework health
qa-project-context.md OR existing documentation
local environment setup OR setup duration
accounts and permissions OR repository access OR CI dashboard
anti_patterns: skip discovery and jump to planning
assume everyone has same framework experience

evals.prompt: Describe the pairing strategy for the first three tests a new QA engineer writes. What is the progression?
expected_patterns: test 1 OR first test: navigator OR driver OR experienced person explains
test 2 OR second test: co-pilots OR equal contribution
test 3 OR third test: observer OR new person drives OR feedback only
understand the workflow OR build confidence OR independence
pair on first 3 tests OR 2-week review cycle
anti_patterns: same pairing pattern for all three tests
observer role for test 1 instead of test 3

evals.prompt: Our new tester is stuck on their first test PR. What should code review focus on during the first two weeks?
expected_patterns: pattern adherence OR page object usage
selector strategy OR stable locators
assertion quality OR specific assertions
test isolation OR shared state
naming OR test name describes behavior
educational comments OR explain the why
supportive review, not just LGTM
anti_patterns: perfectionism OR blocking on advanced patterns
focus only on syntax, not understanding
treat first PRs same as experienced team member PRs

evals.prompt: Our project has no qa-project-context.md file. Why is this important for onboarding and what should it contain?
expected_patterns: documents framework OR critical paths OR team structure
tribal knowledge OR document what they learn
new person.*document OR benefits the entire team
blocking item OR high-priority task
team_maturity setting OR startup OR growing OR established
forces documentation of what was learned
anti_patterns: created by team member, not new person

evals.prompt: What is the most important metric for measuring onboarding success? Why?
expected_patterns: time to first merged test OR first real test
within the first two weeks OR first 2 weeks
merged into the main branch OR runs in CI
not a tutorial exercise OR not local only
reduces time to first merged test OR time to productive

evals.prompt: Identify the anti-patterns that indicate failed QA onboarding. What should we avoid?
expected_patterns: sink or swim OR no structure
tutorial-only OR no real tests early
no documentation OR all tribal knowledge
perfectionism paralysis OR blocking first PR
ignoring the onboarding experience OR soliciting feedback
copy-paste without understanding OR code review doesn't explain why
anti_patterns: all anti-patterns listed as isolated issues
no explanation of why these are problems

evals.prompt: How should we structure the Codebase Orientation Guide session? What areas should we cover?
expected_patterns: 60-90 minute session OR walk-through duration
test directory structure OR organization by feature
page objects tour OR naming conventions OR base page class
shared utilities inventory OR auth fixture OR data factory OR API client
CI pipeline walk-through OR stages OR triggers OR artifacts
Why tests are organized this way OR where to find things
anti_patterns: reading documentation instead of walking through
focus only on code structure, not purpose
no table OR utilities not explained
